question: Which state is in both of these groups: the states ranked 8th through 12th by the average share of covered medical costs a plan is expected to pay, reported in 2022, and the states are ranked 5th through 9th by the median reported number of foreclosures under Senate Bill 558 in 2016? Respond with [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/plan-attributes-puf-py2022/files/plan_attributes_puf.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AK": "Alaska", "AL": "Alabama", "AR": "Arkansas", "AZ": "Arizona",
    "CA": "California", "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DC": "District of Columbia",
    "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia", "HI": "Hawaii",
    "IA": "Iowa", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "MA": "Massachusetts",
    "MD": "Maryland", "ME": "Maine", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota",
    "MO": "Missouri", "MS": "Mississippi", "MT": "Montana", "NC": "North Carolina",
    "ND": "North Dakota", "NE": "Nebraska", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NV": "Nevada", "NY": "New York", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island",
    "SC": "South Carolina", "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas",
    "UT": "Utah", "VA": "Virginia", "VT": "Vermont", "WA": "Washington",
    "WI": "Wisconsin", "WV": "West Virginia", "WY": "Wyoming"
}

df = df.copy()
df["normalized_state"] = df["StateCode"].map(state_map)
df["IssuerActuarialValue"] = pd.to_numeric(
    df["IssuerActuarialValue"].astype(str).str.strip().str.rstrip("%"),
    errors="coerce"
)

state_metric = (
    df.dropna(subset=["normalized_state", "IssuerActuarialValue"])
      .groupby("normalized_state", as_index=False)["IssuerActuarialValue"]
      .mean()
      .rename(columns={"IssuerActuarialValue": "avg_issuer_actuarial_value"})
)

ranked = state_metric.sort_values(
    ["avg_issuer_actuarial_value", "normalized_state"],
    ascending=[False, True]
).reset_index(drop=True)

result = ranked.iloc[7:12][["normalized_state", "avg_issuer_actuarial_value"]]
answer = result["normalized_state"].tolist()
1.subquestion: Which states are ranked 8th through 12th by the average share of covered medical costs a plan is expected to pay in 2022?

2.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2017-foreclosure-avoidance-mediation-beneficiary-exemption-affidavits-pursuant-to-sb-558/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AK": "Alaska", "AL": "Alabama", "AR": "Arkansas", "AZ": "Arizona",
    "CA": "California", "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DC": "District of Columbia",
    "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia", "HI": "Hawaii",
    "IA": "Iowa", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "MA": "Massachusetts",
    "MD": "Maryland", "ME": "Maine", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota",
    "MO": "Missouri", "MS": "Mississippi", "MT": "Montana", "NC": "North Carolina",
    "ND": "North Dakota", "NE": "Nebraska", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NV": "Nevada", "NY": "New York", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island",
    "SC": "South Carolina", "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas",
    "UT": "Utah", "VA": "Virginia", "VT": "Vermont", "WA": "Washington",
    "WI": "Wisconsin", "WV": "West Virginia", "WY": "Wyoming"
}

df = df.copy()
df["normalized_state"] = df["State"].astype(str).str.strip().map(state_map)
df["Foreclosures previous year"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Foreclosures previous year"], errors="coerce")

state_metric = (
    df.dropna(subset=["normalized_state", "Foreclosures previous year"])
      .groupby("normalized_state", as_index=False)["Foreclosures previous year"]
      .median()
      .rename(columns={"Foreclosures previous year": "median_foreclosures_previous_year"})
)

ranked = state_metric.sort_values(
    ["median_foreclosures_previous_year", "normalized_state"],
    ascending=[False, True]
).reset_index(drop=True)

result = ranked.iloc[4:9][["normalized_state", "median_foreclosures_previous_year"]]
answer = result["normalized_state"].tolist()
2.subquestion: Which states are ranked 5th through 9th by the median reported number of foreclosures under Senate Bill 558 in 2016?

reasoning: Node 1: Normalize `StateCode` to full state names, convert `IssuerActuarialValue` to numeric, compute the mean `IssuerActuarialValue` by state, sort by that mean descending with state name ascending as the tiebreaker, and take ranks 8 through 12.

Node 2: Trim whitespace from `State`, normalize it to full state names, convert `Foreclosures previous year` to numeric, compute the median `Foreclosures previous year` by state, sort by that median descending with state name ascending as the tiebreaker, and take ranks 5 through 9.
Hop result: semantic intersection of <node 1 answer> and <node 2 answer> = Wyoming.
